title: Module 6.5: International Outdoor Standards - Assessment
description: Assessment of knowledge for international outdoor laser safety standards

questions.text: Which international standard provides guidance specifically for laser emitters and flight safety?
questions.explanation: ICAO Doc 9817 is specifically titled 'Manual on Laser Emitters and Flight Safety' and provides international guidance on this topic.

questions.text: Calculate the NOHD for a 40W CW visible laser with 1.5 mrad divergence. Use MPE = 2.6 mW/cm².
What is the primary difference between the FAA and EASA regulatory approaches?

options.text: FAA is prescriptive, EASA is performance-based

questions.explanation: The FAA uses a prescriptive approach with specific requirements, while EASA uses a performance-based approach emphasizing operator risk assessment.

questions.text: A laser emits 10W at 520nm (green). What is the Visually Corrected Power? (VCF for 520nm = 0.7092)
questions.explanation: VCP = Power × VCF = 10W × 0.7092 = 7.092W. The green laser appears less bright than its actual power would suggest.

questions.text: Which UK legislation specifically addresses aiming lasers at aircraft?

options.text: Health and Safety at Work Act

questions.explanation: The UK Laser Misuse Act 2018 specifically makes it an offense to shine a laser at aircraft, with penalties up to 5 years imprisonment.

questions.text: If the SZED is 10,000 feet, what is the LFED?
What is the maximum irradiance allowed in the Critical Flight Zone (CFZ)?
questions.explanation: The CFZ limit is 5 µW/cm². The LFZ is 50 nW/cm² and the SFZ is 100 µW/cm².

questions.text: An RGB laser has 8W red (638nm), 4W green (520nm), and 10W blue (445nm). What is the overall VCF?
Which country requires a minimum of £5M public liability insurance for outdoor laser operations?
questions.explanation: The UK CAA requires a minimum of £5M public liability insurance for outdoor laser operations under CAP 736.

questions.text: Calculate the CA correction factor for a laser at 850nm.
Which of the following are SAE Aerospace Standards referenced in FAA AC 70-1B? (Select all that apply)

options.text: ARP5293A - Safety Considerations for Lasers in Airspace
AS6029B - Performance Criteria for Control Measures

questions.explanation: The SAE standards referenced are ARP5290, ARP5293A, ARP5535, AS4970, and AS6029B. AS9001 is a quality management standard, not a laser safety standard.

questions.text: ICAO standards are legally binding on all member states.
questions.explanation: ICAO provides guidance and recommended practices, but they are not legally binding unless adopted into national law by each member state.

questions.text: The FAA's prescriptive outdoor laser guidance is found in Advisory Circular ______.
questions.explanation: FAA AC 70-1B is the primary guidance document for outdoor laser operations in the United States.

questions.text: A laser show tour will visit Germany, France, and the UK. Which regulatory requirement is MOST RESTRICTIVE?
questions.explanation: While all are important, the UK's £5M insurance requirement and mandatory CAA-registered LSO are typically the most restrictive requirements for international tours.

questions.text: Match the regulatory body with its jurisdiction:
questions.explanation: Understanding which regulatory body has jurisdiction is essential for international operations.
